mood_reading.summary: You're after a proper chill-out, a bit of peace and quiet away from the hustle. Just you, the water, and maybe a gentle breeze. No crowds, no fuss, just a good old calm dip.

plan.where: Head on over to Malabar Beach, down in the Eastern Suburbs. It's a bit of a local secret, so it keeps things nice and relaxed.
plan.when: Any time outside of peak school holiday madness or a scorching weekend arvo is usually pretty good for a quiet swim here. Mid-morning or afternoon is ideal for a proper squiz.
plan.why: This spot is perfect for a solo, calm swim. The bay keeps the water nice and flat, so you can just float about and forget the world. It’s got a real community feel without being overrun.
plan.conditions_summary: The water's sitting at a comfy 20.6 degrees, so it's not too chilly. There's a light breeze, but being a protected bay, it won't kick up too much fuss on the water itself. UV is moderate, so chuck on some sunscreen.
plan.gentle_warning: Just a heads-up, like with any bay beach, it's best to avoid swimming after heavy rain. The water quality can drop a bit then, so keep an eye on local alerts if it's been bucketing down.

rejected_beaches.name: Obelisk Beach
rejected_beaches.reason: Obelisk is a cracker for seclusion and views, but it's a bit of a mission from Newtown on public transport, and it's clothing optional, which might not be what you're after for a simple quiet swim.

rejected_beaches.name: Silver Beach

mood_phrase: I want a beach day near Little Congwong Beach.

mood_reading.summary: You're after a proper chill-out day, just you and the ocean. Sounds like you want to escape the hustle and just soak up some calm, maybe with a good book or just your thoughts. A bit of quiet solitude is on the cards, yeah?

plan.where: Little Congwong Beach, down in La Perouse. It's a bit of a mission from Avalon, but totally worth the trip for the peace and quiet.
plan.when: Best to head out early to beat any potential arvo crowds, especially since you're coming from Avalon. Give yourself plenty of travel time, it's a bit of a trek but worth it for the proper solitude.
plan.why: This spot is proper serene. It's protected, so the water's usually calm, perfect for a quiet dip or just sitting by the bay. You can really switch off here and just enjoy the moment.
plan.conditions_summary: The water's a pleasant 20.5°C, with a light breeze at 13.8 km/h from the south-southeast. UV is moderate at 4.35, so slap on some sunscreen. Waves are pretty mellow, as you'd expect in a protected bay.
plan.gentle_warning: It's a bit of a walk to get down there from the main road, so wear comfy shoes. And remember, facilities are pretty basic, so come prepared with everything you need for your chill-out session.

rejected_beaches.name: Frenchmans Beach
rejected_beaches.reason: Right next door to Little Congwong, but it tends to attract more families and can be a bit livelier. You're after proper quiet, and Little Congwong delivers that better for your solo day.

mood_phrase: I want a beach day near Camp Cove.

mood_reading.summary: Sounds like you're after a proper chill-out, just you and the harbour, right near your preferred spot. No fuss, just good vibes and a bit of a squiz at the water.

plan.where: Head straight to Camp Cove in Watsons Bay. You can't miss it, it's a cracker of a spot right there.
plan.when: Anytime you like, but the arvo is usually pretty good for a bit of sun and calm. Just dodge the peak school holiday rush if you're after true tranquility.
plan.why: It's exactly what you asked for – a proper harbour beach day, perfect for unwinding solo. You'll get those lovely calm waters, a bit of shade, and a kiosk if you fancy a snack. It's got everything you need for a no-stress day by the water.
bring: Sunscreen (even if it's a bit cooler)
Your swimmers (if you're brave enough for a quick dip)
A picnic lunch or some cash for the kiosk
plan.conditions_summary: The water's a bit on the fresh side at 18.5°C, so maybe not a full send into the waves, but still fine for a quick dip if you're keen. Wind's a gentle 14.4 km/h, so it won't be blowing your hat off.
plan.gentle_warning: Just a heads-up, Camp Cove can get a bit busy, especially on a nice day. If you're after absolute solitude, maybe aim for a weekday. And always keep an eye on your belongings, mate.

plan.when: Head down whenever you fancy this arvo, it's your local and perfect for a relaxed solo session.

plan.conditions_summary: It's a bit cooler today, around 18 degrees, with a moderate southerly breeze at about 17 km/h. Waves are around 1.2 metres, so it's good for a squiz or a walk along the sand. UV is low, so no need to fry.

mood_phrase: I want a beach day near Elouera Beach.

mood_reading.summary: Sounds like you're after a proper chill-out day, just you and the sand. No need for big crowds or a huge party scene, just some peace and quiet near Elouera.

plan.where: Elouera Beach, Cronulla. It's right there in the Shire, easy to find.
plan.when: Head down in the morning to beat any potential crowds and really stretch out that relaxed feeling. The arvo might get a bit breezy.
plan.why: It's perfect for a solo mission when you just want to clear your head. The wide-open sand means you won't feel squashed, and you can just wander along the shore at your own pace. With the waves up, it's a great spot to watch the surfers and just breathe it all in.
bring: Headphones for tunes or a podcast
plan.conditions_summary: It's a bit cool at 18.2°C, with a decent southerly breeze at 15.9 km/h. The waves are a solid 1.84m, so it's more for a good walk and watching the surf than a calm dip. UV is low, so no need to stress too much about getting burnt, but still slip, slop, slap.
plan.gentle_warning: Just a heads-up, those waves are a bit punchy today at 1.84m. If you're keen for a swim, stick between the flags and have a squiz at what the lifeguards are saying. Rips can be a bit sneaky out there, so be smart.

plan.when: Anytime really, but a mid-morning or arvo dip is usually spot on for a relaxed vibe when you're after a bit of peace.
plan.why: It's a sheltered bay, so the water's usually super calm, perfect for a cruisy swim. Plus, it's not usually packed, so you'll get that peace you're after. Proper low-key.

mood_reading.summary: You're after a proper chill-out, a bit of peace and quiet with the family, without too much fuss. Laidback is the name of the game, yeah?

plan.where: Shelly Beach, just a lovely, scenic stroll from Manly Wharf. You can't miss it.
plan.when: Head over in the morning, before it gets too busy, or later in the arvo for a bit more peace. Weekdays are always a bit more chill, if you can swing it.
plan.why: It's got that beautiful protected cove, so it's usually flat as a tack, perfect for the family to relax without worrying about big waves. You can snorkel, have a gentle paddle, or just kick back on the sand. Plus, the walk there from Manly is proper nice.
bring: Swimmers (even if it's a bit brisk, worth a dip!)
Snorkel gear if you're keen for a squiz at the fish
A picnic – there's a kiosk, but a packed lunch is always a good shout
plan.conditions_summary: The water's a bit cool at 18.9°C, but the wind's barely a whisper at 2.1 km/h, so it'll feel pretty calm. UV is low, so no need to stress too much about getting burnt, but still slip, slop, slap. While there are some waves out in the open ocean, Shelly's protected cove will be much calmer.
plan.gentle_warning: Even though it's protected, always keep a proper eye on the little ones, especially near the water. And remember, it can get a bit busy on sunny weekends, so an early start is a good idea for a truly laidback experience.

plan.when: Best to go during the day, maybe mid-morning, to catch the best light and avoid any potential late-arvo breezes. UV is low, so that's a bonus.
plan.why: This little bay is a cracker for a solo swim when you want to escape. It's got that 'secret spot' feel, and while the waves are up a bit, the protected bay should still let you have a decent, quiet paddle.
bring: sunscreen (even with low UV, best to be safe)
plan.conditions_summary: It's a mild 18.1°C in the water, with a light breeze around 10.5 km/h from the southwest. UV is super low, so no need to worry about getting cooked. The main thing to note is the wave height is a bit up at 2.08m, even in the bay. So, take it easy.
plan.gentle_warning: With those waves a bit higher than usual for a bay, just take a squiz before you jump in. It's still a protected spot, but always best to be aware. No lifeguards here, so swim within your limits, yeah?

mood_phrase: I want a beach day near Cobblers Beach.

mood_reading.summary: You're after a proper chill-out day, just you and the harbour ripples. Somewhere quiet, a bit secluded, where you can just breathe and forget the city buzz. A bit of a wander and a quiet dip sounds like the go.

plan.where: Cobblers Beach, Mosman. You'll find it tucked away in Sydney Harbour National Park.
plan.when: Head out a bit earlier in the arvo to make the most of the quiet and the light for your walk.
plan.why: It's a top spot for a bit of solitude and a proper relax. You can have a squiz at the harbour views, maybe a little dip, or just find a comfy spot to read your book.
plan.conditions_summary: It's a bit breezy out there with a moderate wind from the southeast at 15.3 km/h, and the water's a cool 18.3°C. The waves are up a bit for a harbour spot, around 1.9m, so it might be a bit choppy for a full-send swim, but still fine for a paddle. UV is low at 2.4.
plan.gentle_warning: Getting there by public transport from Arncliffe will be a bit of a journey, mate. You'll likely need a bus or two, plus a decent walk through the bushland to get to the sand. Plan your route and give yourself plenty of time, it's worth the trip for the seclusion.

rejected_beaches.reason: Yeah nah, Silver Beach is closer and good for public transport, but with those 2.5m waves, it's going to be a bit too wild for a relaxing swim today. Not the calm vibe you're after.

rejected_beaches.name: Gordons Bay
rejected_beaches.reason: It's a nice spot, but the waves are pretty high at 2.2m, which isn't ideal for a calm, relaxing dip. Plus, it can get a bit busier than what you're looking for today.

rejected_beaches.name: Little Congwong Beach
rejected_beaches.reason: Can't give you the lowdown on this one, mate. The live conditions data is playing hard to get, so it's a no-go for a recommendation today. Safety first, you know.

rejected_beaches.name: Yarra Bay Beach
rejected_beaches.reason: Same deal as Little Congwong, mate. No live conditions means no recommendation from me. Can't send you off without knowing what's happening.

mood_phrase: I want a beach day near Cronulla Beach.

mood_reading.summary: Sounds like you're after a proper cruisy day out, just you and the beach, with that familiar Cronulla feel. No fuss, just good vibes.

plan.where: Head straight to Cronulla Beach. It's the heart of the Cronulla strip, so you can't miss it. Perfect for a solo mission.
plan.when: Anytime you fancy, really. The arvo could be nice for a stroll and a coffee, or pop down earlier if you want to beat any potential crowds for your quiet time.
plan.why: You can grab a coffee, find a spot to chill and watch the waves, or just take a proper long walk along the sand. It's got a great vibe for just unwinding and soaking it all in, without needing to be super social.
bring: Money for a coffee or a feed
plan.conditions_summary: It's a bit on the cooler side today, around 16.7°C, with a light breeze. The waves are a bit chunky at 2.62m, so it's more for watching than a proper swim. Good walking weather, though!
plan.gentle_warning: With those waves being a bit high, probably best to keep out of the water for a proper swim today. Stick to the sand and enjoy the fresh air.

rejected_beaches.name: Wanda Beach
rejected_beaches.reason: Wanda's a bit further down the track and more for a wilder, less accessible vibe. Not quite the 'near Cronulla Beach' feel you're after, and a bit of a trek without the train access.

rejected_beaches.name: Elouera Beach
rejected_beaches.reason: Elouera's decent for a walk, but it's not as central for cafes and that easy-going 'beach day' feel right in the thick of it like Cronulla Beach. Plus, the waves are up there too.

rejected_beaches.name: North Cronulla Beach
rejected_beaches.reason: North Cronulla is right next door, yeah, but Cronulla Beach itself just edges it out for that classic, easy-access, 'I'm here to relax' vibe you're after, especially with the cafes right there.

mood_reading.summary: Sounds like you're after a proper chill-out session, just you and the waves. A bit of 'lalala' in your step, wanting somewhere calm and easy to unwind. No fuss, just good vibes.

plan.where: Head out to Little Congwong Beach, mate. It's tucked away in La Perouse, a real gem for a bit of solitude.
plan.when: Best to aim for a weekday morning if you can swing it. You'll get the place mostly to yourself before anyone else thinks about it.
plan.why: Because you want to relax, proper. This spot is calm, protected, and just the ticket for a quiet swim or a bit of sun-soaking without the usual crowds. It's got that 'get away from it all' feel, even though it's still Sydney.
plan.conditions_summary: Today's looking pretty cruisy down there. Water temp is a decent 18.2°C, and the wind's barely a whisper at 2.9 km/h. UV is low, so no need to stress too much about getting burnt. The waves are a bit up out on the open ocean, but Little Congwong is a protected bay, so it should be nice and calm for a dip.
plan.gentle_warning: Just a heads-up, it's a bit of a walk from the bus stop and there aren't many facilities right on the sand. So, pack everything you need for your chill-out session. And remember, it's a bit more 'au naturel' down there sometimes, if you catch my drift, so be prepared for that.

rejected_beaches.name: Yarra Bay Beach
rejected_beaches.reason: Yarra Bay is closer, yeah, and it's a calm spot, but it's more of a local family beach. For a proper solo 'lalala' relax, Little Congwong just edges it out with that secluded vibe.

rejected_beaches.name: Silver Beach
rejected_beaches.reason: Silver Beach is pretty good, and the water's a touch warmer, but it's known for being dog-friendly and a bit more for families. Not quite the solo escape you're after.

rejected_beaches.name: Malabar Beach
rejected_beaches.reason: Malabar's usually a good calm bay, but the water's a bit cooler today, and the conditions aren't quite as inviting for a swim as other spots.

rejected_beaches.name: Gordons Bay
rejected_beaches.reason: Gordons Bay is stunning, proper beautiful, but it can get a bit busy, and it's more for snorkelling and couples. Not quite the right fit for a solo chill-out.
